items.headline: U.S. Equities Retreat from Record Highs on Inflation Fears and Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: U.S. equities experienced broad selling pressure on Friday, reversing recent optimism, as investors adopted a cautious stance. Rising Treasury yields, higher crude oil prices, and persistent inflation concerns weighed heavily on market sentiment. The closely watched summit between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping concluded without major breakthroughs, further contributing to the cautious mood. Technology stocks, especially those in the AI sector, led the declines as traders engaged in profit-taking after a robust rally. The CBOE Volatility Index surged, signaling expectations for continued market fluctuations.

items.statContext: Down 92.74 points (-1.2%) on Friday. Up 0.1% for the week.
Down 410.08 points (-1.5%) on Friday. Down 0.1% for the week.
Down 537.29 points (-1.1%) on Friday. Down 0.2% for the week.

items.headline: Treasury Yields Hit One-Year High Amid Inflation Fears
items.summary: The benchmark 10-year U.S. Treasury yield rose to 4.56%-4.60%, its highest level in nearly a year, driven by hotter-than-expected April inflation data and rising geopolitical risks. The 30-year Treasury yield also climbed above 5.1%.

items.headline: Crude Oil Prices Surge Above $100 Per Barrel
items.summary: WTI crude oil prices surged to over $100-$105.90 per barrel, marking an 11.85% increase for the week, with Brent crude futures climbing 3%. This rise is largely attributed to the ongoing conflict in Iran and continued restrictions on traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, fueling inflation concerns.

items.headline: April Inflation Data Shows Persistent Pressures
items.summary: April's Consumer Price Index (CPI) accelerated to +3.8% year-over-year and +0.6% month-over-month. Core CPI, excluding volatile food and energy prices, rose 2.8%. The Producer Price Index (PPI) surged even higher, by +6.0% year-over-year and +1.4% month-over-month, surprising analysts and indicating rising business costs.

items.headline: Jobless Claims Tick Up, Retail Sales Meet Expectations
items.summary: Initial jobless claims for the week ended May 9 increased by 12,000 to 211,000, remaining within the 200,000 to 250,000 range seen since 2022. Meanwhile, retail sales in April grew +0.5%, aligning with market expectations.

items.headline: Kevin Warsh Confirmed as New Federal Reserve Chair
items.summary: Kevin Warsh has been confirmed as the new Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Jerome Powell will serve as chair pro tempore until Warsh is officially sworn in. Warsh assumes leadership at a challenging time, facing a tenuously stable labor market, reaccelerating inflation, and a divided Fed Board on monetary policy direction.

items.headline: Q1 Earnings Season Nears End with Strong Growth
items.summary: The first-quarter earnings season for S&P 500 companies is largely concluded, with approximately 91% of companies having reported results. The blended earnings growth rate stands at a robust 27.7%, significantly outpacing initial estimates.

items.headline: Cisco Reports Strong Q3 Amid AI Demand
items.summary: Cisco Systems ($CSCO) announced strong third-quarter fiscal 2026 non-GAAP earnings of $1.06 per share, surpassing estimates. Revenues reached $15.841 billion, driven by substantial demand for the company's products to support the significant growth of AI-powered network traffic.

items.headline: Upcoming Earnings: Nvidia, Walmart, Home Depot
items.summary: Next week will feature key earnings reports from major companies. Nvidia ($NVDA) is set to release its Q1 2027 fiscal results. Retail giants Walmart ($WMT) and Home Depot ($HD) will also provide insights into consumer health and spending trends.

items.headline: Trump-Xi Summit Concludes Without Major Breakthroughs
items.summary: The highly anticipated summit between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping concluded in Beijing without the announcement of any major trade agreements or policy breakthroughs, contributing to investor caution in the market.

items.headline: U.S. National Debt Exceeds GDP, Economists Call it 'Symbolic'
items.summary: The U.S. national debt has surpassed the size of the American economy, with a debt-to-GDP ratio of 100.2% as of March 2026. While a notable milestone, Northeastern University economists largely consider this development symbolic rather than an immediate economic crisis.

items.headline: Energy Sector Leads as Tech Lags on Friday
items.summary: On Friday, the Energy sector emerged as the top-performing sector, buoyed by surging crude oil prices. Conversely, the Information Technology sector, particularly AI-related stocks, led the declines as investors engaged in profit-taking. For the week, while Information Technology and Communication Services were among leading sectors, Consumer Discretionary and Real Estate showed weakness.
